p.em: Proceed in your native language if you wish. Instructions will remain in English.

ut:template.p: If all actions produce the expected results described, please submit a 'passed' result.
If any action fails, or produces an unexpected result, please submit a 'failed' result and file a bug. Please be sure to include the bug number when you submit your result.

ut:configuration.ut:test:
Test-case Live Session Start
Boot up the image FAMILY boot screen is displayed When the installer starts select your language in the left column Language is selected, all labels are changed to translated versions Click on 'Continue' The 'Try or Install' screen is displayed with 'try FAMILY' and 'Install FAMILY' buttons Click on the 'Try FAMILY' icon to select the option and click on the 'Continue' button The default desktop is displayed
Test-case Live Session Usage
Use and execute the default applications found for the desktop environment being run All applications should function without error

ut:test.dt: Try installing on a machine with a Windows installation and a bitlocker partition
Boot up the image in UEFI mode
In GRUB boot menu, select UEFI Firmware settings
Check hardware settings for the hard-drive and if set to ACPI mode, change it to "RAID" or "RST"
Save and exit the firmware
Boot up the image
ut:test.dd: If you see the GRUB boot menu you should see the following:

ut:test.dt: Select the 'Try or Install FAMILY' option and press Enter
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly and loads the installer displaying the Welcome dialog with language selection and the mascot logo on the right.'

ut:test.dt: Select the 'FAMILY (safe graphics)' option and press Enter
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly and loads the installer displaying the Welcome dialog with language selection and the mascot logo on the right.'

ut:test.dt: Click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The 'Try or Install' screen is displayed with 'try FAMILY' and 'Install FAMILY' buttons
ut:test.dt: Click on the release notes hyperlink to confirm that a browser launches and you are taken to the release notes discourse page.
Click on the 'Install FAMILY' icon to select the option and click on the Continue button
ut:test.dd: The 'Keyboard layout' screen appears
The proposed keyboard corresponds with your keyboard
ut:test.dt: Select your keyboard layout and click on Continue
ut:test.dd: The 'Connect to a network' screen is displayed
The screen should reflect the current status and display those elements

dd.li: Connect to a Wi-Fi network followed by a scrollable list of available APs, displaying an active one colored with a leading checkmark
Connect to a hidden Wi-Fi network
I don't want to connect to internet for now

ut:test.dt: Click on 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'Applications and updates' screen is displayed, listing normal and minimal installation, as well as options for installing updates, third party software and additional media formats.

ut:test.dt: Click on 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'Installation type' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Select 'Manual Partitioning' and click 'Next'
ut:test.dd: A screen showing the current hard disks and partition layouts is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select the drive you wish to partition and use the Add '+', Change 'Change', and Delete '-' buttons to create your desired scheme
ut:test.dd: The screen updates showing your desired partitions and mount points
ut:test.dt: Once you have your required partitioning scheme laid out, click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The 'Write changes to disk' screen is displayed, including the details of incoming partitions changes.
ut:test.dt: Verify that the partitioning details make sense
Click 'Start Installing'
Select 'Something Else' and click 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: A screen showing the current hard disks and partition layouts is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select the drive you wish to partition and use the Add '+', Change 'Change', and Delete '-' buttons to create your desired scheme
ut:test.dd: The screen updates showing your desired partitions and mount points
ut:test.em: Make sure that your scheme also includes a separate /home partition
ut:test.dt: Once you have your required partitioning scheme laid out, click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The 'Write changes to disk' screen is displayed, including the details of incoming partitions changes.
ut:test.dt: Verify that the partitioning details make sense
Click 'Start Installing'
Select 'Install FAMILY XX.XX alongside SYSTEM YY'
ut:test.dd: (SYSTEM YY is the name of the system already installed on disk (FAMILY 12.04, Windows 7, ...)
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The Screen Install FAMILY XX.XX alongside SYSTEM YY appears
ut:test.dt: Note the drive selected on the Select drive list and the bar state
ut:test.strong: If the target drive has a free partition with sufficient free space, install will proceed without further partitioning intervention
ut:test.dd: The drive corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda) and the bar is divided
ut:test.dt: Move the bar that separates the sections as appropriate
ut:test.dd: The slider bar can be set as appropriate
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Install Now' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Write changes to disk' screen is displayed, including the details of incoming partitions changes.
ut:test.dt: Verify that the partitioning details make sense
Click 'Start Installing'
Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The 'Write changes to disk' screen is displayed, including the details of incoming partitions changes.
ut:test.dt: Verify that the partitioning details make sense
ut:test.dd: The available disk should be used for the installation
ut:test.dt: Click 'Start Installing'
Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY installation' and check 'Encrypt the new FAMILY installation for security'
ut:test.dd: 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY Installation' is selected and 'Encrypt the new FAMILY Installation for security' is checked
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'LVM and encryption selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: The 'Choose a security key' screen is displayed with the 'Cotinue' button greyed out
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type a different key into the 'confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The installer displays 'The security keys do not match'
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type the same key into the 'Confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The passwords are accepted and the 'Continue' button can be clicked
ut:test.dt: Click the 'Show security key' checkbox
ut:test.dd: The box is checked and the passwords are displayed
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Continue'
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Verify that the partitioning details make sense
Click 'Start installing'
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed
The 'Turn off BitLocker' screen is displayed and there is no way to continue the installation
ut:test.dt: Scan the QR code with a phone
ut:test.dd: Verify that the page help.ubuntu.com/rst opens with detailed information about RST and how to disable it.
ut:test.dt: Follow the instructions on the page OR disable RST from UEFI firmware

ut:test.dd: The 'Turn off RST' screen is displayed and there is no way to continue the installation
ut:test.dt: Scan the QR code with a phone
ut:test.dd: Verify that the page help.ubuntu.com/rst opens with detailed information about RST and how to disable it.
ut:test.dt: Follow the instructions on the page OR disable RST from UEFI firmware

ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed
ut:test.dt: If your system is connected to the network, note the preselected timezone corresponds with your timezone and the city indicated in the text box
ut:test.dd: The timezone and city displayed match your timezone and a major city from your area
ut:test.dt: Select your timezone, and click on the Continue button
ut:test.dd: The 'Who are you?' screen appears
ut:test.dt: Input your initial user details and password admin can not be used - it is a dedicated Linux User
ut:test.dd: Name, username and password are accepted.
ut:test.dt: The "Require my password to log in" slider is already highlighted.
ut:test.dd: The "Use Active Directory" tickbox is unchecked.
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'Choose your theme' screen is displayed showing light and dark options, as well as color options below.
ut:test.dt: Click on 'Next'
Wait for the installer to finish
ut:test.dd: An 'Installation Complete' dialog appears
ut:test.dt: Click the 'Restart Now' button
ut:test.dd: GUI is shut down, a prompt to remove media and press Enter appears
ut:test.dt: Remove the disc and press enter
ut:test.dd: The machine is rebooted

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly and loads into FAMILY showing username selected

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly
A text entry is displayed to enter your password to decrypt the disk
The system loads into FAMILY showing username selected

ut:test.dt: Click on "Advanced features"
ut:test.dd: Click on the tickbox for "Enhanced secure-boot"
Click on the tickbox for Hardware-backed full disk encryption
Click "Ok"
ut:test.dt: Click Continue
Click on "Advanced features"
ut:test.dd: There should be no tickbox for "Enhanced secure-boot"
Click "Ok"
ut:test.dt: Click Continue
Click on "Advanced features"
Verify there is no option for Hardware-backed full disk encryption with TPM.
For this check, please use a system with no TPM enabled hardware
For this check, please use a system with TPM enabled hardware, with approx 20G of disk space. The install should fail later on.
For this check, please use a system with TPM enabled hardware, with $REASONITWILLFAIL.
After rebooting, log in to the installed system and run `snap list`, and check that the `pc-kernel` package is installed.
Open up a terminal window, and try to run this command: `sudo apt install linux-image-generic`.
ut:test.dd: You should get a response which includes this message: `boot-managed-by-snapd : Conflicts: linux-image`

ut:test.dt: In order to do full disk encryption with TPM, you either need to make sure you're using a piece of hardware that has TPM functionality, or you can install with a VM, making sure you have the correct options set to emulate a TPM backed system.
Somewhere along the installation process, either in the install screen or after reboot, the install should fail.
Upon reaching the desktop environment, you should be greeted with the "Choose your language" screen.
ut:test.dd: Pick your desired language.

ut:test.dt: Login, and check the following command runs without any errors:
ut:test.dd: cat /home/$USER/dummy_file

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Erase disk and use ZFS'
ut:test.dd: 'Erase disk and use ZFS' is selected
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'ZFS selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Install Now' button
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue

If there is only one hard disk, skip to the 'Where are you?' screen. Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' screen verify that the drive selected on the Select drive list corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda)
ut:test.dd: Selected drive is displayed on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Next button
Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly
The system loads into FAMILY showing username selected
Upon login, open a terminal, run the following commands and verify it matches the output

dd.code: $ zfs mount | sort bpool/BOOT/ubuntu_UUID /boot rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID / rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/srv /srv rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/usr/local /usr/local rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/games /var/games rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/AccountsService /var/lib/AccountsService rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/apt /var/lib/apt rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/dpkg /var/lib/dpkg rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/NetworkManager /var/lib/NetworkManager rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib /var/lib rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/log /var/log rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/mail /var/mail rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/snap /var/snap rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/spool /var/spool rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/www /var/www rpool/USERDATA/root_0y7dio /root rpool/USERDATA/u_0y7dio /home/u

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY installation' and check 'Encrypt the new FAMILY installation for security'
ut:test.dd: 'Encrypt the new FAMILY Installation for security' and 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY Installation' is checked
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'LVM and encryption selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Next' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Choose a security key' screen is displayed with the 'Install Now' button greyed out
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type a different key into the 'confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The installer displays 'Passwords do not match'
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type the same key into the 'Confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The passwords are accepted and the 'Next' button can be clicked
ut:test.dt: Click on the Continue button (if there is only one hard disk in the system, the button should read 'Install Now')
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue

If there is only one hard disk, skip to the 'Where are you?' screen. Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' screen verify that the drive selected on the Select drive list corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda)
ut:test.dd: Selected drive is displayed on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Next button
Open a terminal using your FAMILY commands or GUI to do so, in terminal enter df -h and press enter
Terminal echoes the output of the df command
ut:test.dd: Check that / and /home use different partitions

ut:test.dt: Create a file in the /home/$USER/ directory to check for later.
Run the following command to create a file we'll check for later:
ut:test.dd: echo "dummy file" > /home/$USER/dummy_file

ut:test.dt: Shutdown the installed system.
Put the installation medium back in, and boot into the installation of a new system.
ut:test.dd: Do everything as before, but when you get to the manual partitioning screen, make sure to remove the old '/' partition, create a new one, and make sure the partition used for '/home' has also been selected to have '/home' as the mount point (highlight the existing home partition, and click the 'Change' button), but make sure the reformat check box is left unchecked.
ut:test.dt: Continue installation as normal, and reboot once finished.

ut:case.id: 1763_Canary Install (entire disk with lvm and encryption)

ut:case.p: The goal of this test case is to check that localization support is functional during the installation, and that language packs are installed correctly for those languages in the ISO, and that an informational message is shown prompting users to connect to the Internet to fully install language support for those languages not included.
ut:case.dt: Verify that your system is localized and that the calendar shows the regional settings correctly

ut:case.p: This is an installation using the Screen-Reader as done by a visually blind individual. Please cover your monitor, lay it face down or turn it off during this installation. You will require speakers or headphones to hear the screen-reader. As you press Enter to continue on each page, it can take several minutes to advance to the next installer page.
ut:case.dt: Wait to hear the drums and press Alt+Super+S to start the screen-reader
ut:case.dd: The screen reader should be active now and read elements allow a keyboard navigation without screen
If doing this test on a VM, ensure the screen reader is enabled in the vm and not the host system.
The screen reader should still be active

ut:case.p: This test is to ensure that the image boots in UEFI, that the resulting installation can boot in UEFI as well, regardless of network availability and that nVidia proprietary drivers are installed and loaded.
ut:case.dt: Boot up the image in UEFI mode with Secure Boot enabled
Disconnect from any network using the top panel controls.
Check 'Install third-party software for graphics and Wi-Fi hardware'
Click on 'Continue'
ut:case.dd: The 'Configure secure boot' screen is displayed
ut:case.dt: Enter a security key and confirm it
Allow the machine to reboot
ut:case.dd: A blue screen titled 'Perform MOK management' is displayed
ut:case.dt: In the list of commands select 'Enroll MOK'
Select 'Continue'
ut:case.dd: The 'Enroll the key(s)' screen is displayed
ut:case.dt: Select 'Yes' and enter the password you set in the 'Preparing to install FAMILY' page of the installer
ut:case.dd: If the password is correct, the screen 'Perform MOK management' is displayed again
ut:case.dt: Select 'Reboot' and allow the machine to reboot
ut:case.dd: The system boots properly and loads into FAMILY showing username selected

ut:define.name: partition-instruction
ut:define.dt: Ensure that your scheme also has a separated /home partition included
ut:define.dd: You will need the separated /home partition to verify that erasing and reinstalling FAMILY will wipe it out

ut:case.dt: Login as the user you created, or ensure that you are auto-logged in as the user created during setup if you checked the auto-login option
ut:case.dd: The new user's desktop is presented
ut:case.dt: Create a new folder on the desktop by right clicking on it and title it 'test', open Firefox and browse to a couple of sites
ut:case.dd: Now you have a test folder on the desktop and some browsing history, this will be needed to check if erasing and reinstall of FAMILY will wipe them out
ut:case.dt: Reboot using the iso with a CD/DVD or USB Key to perform a reinstall of FAMILY

ut:define.name: partition-instruction
ut:define.dt: Use the same /home partition than in the first installation and unsure the format checkbox is not selected
ut:define.dd: You will need the separated /home partition to verify that erasing and reinstalling FAMILY will wipe it out

ut:case.id: 1773_Canary Non-English Installation Online and CJK Input
ut:case.p: The goal of this test case is to check that localization support is functional during the installation, that language packs are downloaded and installed correctly from the Internet and that the input methods for either Chinese, Japanese or Korean are working.
ut:case.dt: Select a CJK language (Chinese, Japanese, Korean)
ut:case.dd: Check that the strings are correctly showing translated and with the right font
ut:case.dt: Verify that your system is localized

Verify that the input method works correctly by entering text in the editor. The input method of choice should be available from the panel and typing in the editor should properly display the ibus chars selector.

ut:case.p: The goal of this test case is to check that the Easy Install option in VMWare Player works correctly with the Ubuntu image.

ut:case.id: 1782_Canary Install no TPM option without TPM support

ut:case.p: The goal of this test case is to check that localization support is functional during the installation, that language packs are downloaded and installed correctly from the Internet and that the input methods for either Chinese, Japanese or Korean are working.
ut:case.dt: Verify that your system is localized

Verify that the input method works correctly by entering text in the editor. The input method of choice should be available from the panel and typing in the editor should properly display the ibus chars selector.

ut:case.id: 1787_Non-English Live Session and Installation; No Network
ut:case.dt: Before doing this test, make sure when booting into the live session you've selected a non-english language. You must also make sure the machine you're installing on has no internet access.
Verify that the language you've selected is being used in the live session.
Verify applications and basic functionality work in the live session, then click the install icon.

ut:case.id: 1788_Non-English Live Session and Installation; Full Network Support
ut:case.dt: Before doing this test, make sure when booting into the live session you've selected a non-english language.
Verify that the language you've selected is being used in the live session.
Verify applications and basic functionality work in the live session, then click the install icon.
For this test, you must boot the iso and after hearing the Ubuntu start up sound, press super+alt+s to activate the screen reader.
After you've activated the screen reader, you must navigate through the install process doing a "Install (Entire Disk)" install, reboot and verify everthing works as intended.
